text.title: Characterization Test Suite Output Contract
text.summary: Defines the shape of a passing characterization-test suite — folder layout, fixture taxonomy, snapshot review record. A suite without this shape is not refactor-ready.

list.item: tests/characterization/ — characterization tests separate from regular unit-test directory.
tests/characterization/fixtures/ — input fixtures, one file per representative scenario, named with origin (production-sample-NNN.json) or scenario (edge-empty-list.json).
tests/characterization/snapshots/ — committed expected outputs; one file per fixture.
tests/characterization/normalize.{py,ts,go} — single normalization module imported by every test.
tests/characterization/README.md — sweep termination criteria; how to update snapshots; reviewer.
fixture-manifest.json: per-fixture origin (production-sample / spec-edge / regression-test), capture date, redaction status.
baseline-signoff.md: reviewer name + ISO date + commit SHA of baseline snapshots.
coverage-report.json: branch coverage at baseline; suite is gated on >= configured threshold.

antipattern.description: Snapshots committed without a baseline-signoff entry.
antipattern.reason: Violates r2 — unreviewed snapshots silently pin bugs as expected behavior.

antipattern.description: Fixtures contain raw PII (emails, names, tokens).
antipattern.reason: Test fixtures live in git forever. Redact at capture-time; record redaction status in manifest.

antipattern.description: CI step auto-updates snapshots on failure (`--update-snapshots` in CI script).
antipattern.reason: Violates r5. Suite becomes a no-op. Update flag must be developer-only, never in CI default.

antipattern.description: Mixing characterization tests with regular unit tests in the same folder.
antipattern.reason: Different semantics. Regular tests test should-behavior; characterization tests test current-behavior. Mixing causes future maintainers to "fix" characterization tests instead of investigating diffs.

list.item: Fixtures captured on pre-refactor branch (verified via git log).
Every snapshot has a corresponding fixture-manifest row.
Normalization module exists and is imported by every snapshot test.
Branch coverage at baseline meets or exceeds the documented threshold.
Baseline-signoff signed by a human; refactor commits start after this date.
